howItWorks.intro: This pregnancy weight gain calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much weight should I gain during pregnancy?
faq.answer: It depends on pre-pregnancy BMI. Normal weight: 25-35 lbs. Underweight: 28-40 lbs. Overweight: 15-25 lbs. Obese: 11-20 lbs. For twins, add 10-15 lbs to these ranges. Most gain happens in the 2nd and 3rd trimesters.

faq.question: Where does pregnancy weight go?
faq.answer: Baby: 7-8 lbs. Placenta: 1.5 lbs. Amniotic fluid: 2 lbs. Uterus: 2 lbs. Breast tissue: 2 lbs. Blood volume: 4 lbs. Fluids: 4 lbs. Fat stores: 5-9 lbs. Total: 27-33 lbs — it all has a purpose.

faq.question: Is it normal to lose weight in the first trimester?
faq.answer: Yes, mild weight loss (1-5 lbs) in the first trimester is common due to morning sickness and appetite changes. This usually doesn't affect the baby. Report weight loss over 5 lbs to your provider. Weight gain typically begins in the second trimester.
